item.title: Xotic XJ-1T 5st -White Blonde / White Pearl Pickguard- #3875 2026 [Made in Japan] — $2,437.46
item.description:
$2,437.46 · Brand New · Kurosawa Music Machida - Japan

Buy with confidence from a trusted Japanese music store!

Kurosawa Music was established in 1957 and has long served as the official distributor of Martin guitars in Japan. We are one of the largest and most respected musical instrument retailers in the country.

Our Machida branch alone has shipped hundreds of guitars and basses to over 50 countries, including the United States, Europe, and across Asia.
Every item is carefully inspected, professionally packed, and shipped with full insurance coverage for your peace of mind.

We are committed to providing safe, reliable service and quality instruments to musicians around the world.
Feel free to contact us anytime with questions — we’re happy to help.

*Caution!!
We do not accept guest checkout of PayPal payment.
Please create your PayPal account before make the payment.

Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask for more details.

Also, please carefully read our shop policy below the guitar description! 


----------------------------------
Body : Ash
Neck : Maple
Fingerboard : Rosewood
Pickups : Raw Vintage w/Wood Cover
Preamp : Xotic Onboard Bass Preamp
Control : 2 Volume, 1 Tone
　　　　3-Band EQ Xotic Onboard Bass Preamp with 2 FQ selector switches
　　　　Neck Volume: Push (Active) / Pull (Passive)
　　　　Bridge Volume: Push (Parallel) / Pull (Series)
　　　　Switch 1 (Mid Frequency Selector): UP/DOWN - 400Hz / 800Hz - ±12dB
　　　　Switch 2 (Treble Frequency Selector): UP/DOWN - 6KHz / 12K - ±18dB


Weight : 4.83kg

With Gig Bag
----------------------------------


Please contact us if you have any questions.
*Notice-

As this product is being sold in retail stores alongside online stores, there is a possibility that it may be sold out.
Please kindly cancel the order if this happens.

Customs Tax

International Buyers Please Note:
Import duties, taxes, and charges are not included in the item price or shipping cost. These charges are the buyer's responsibility.
Please check with your country's customs office to determine what these additional costs will be prior to bidding or buying.
Customs fees are normally charged by the shipping company or collected when you pick the item up. These fees are not additional shipping charges.
We won't under-value merchandise or mark the item as a gift on customs forms. Doing that is against U.S. and international laws.

Shipping
We only ship to PayPal registered address.
Service: EMS or FedEx
Tracking URL: Japan Post, FedEx

Please Note:
We won't under-value merchandise or mark the item as a gift on customs forms.　Doing that is against U.S. and international laws.
We only ship to PayPal registered address.
Please contact us if you reside in countries in which we do not specify shipping service and cost.
Please be aware that delivery could be delayed depending on customs inspection, in case of natural disasters, and other reasons such as postal strikes.

Payment
We only Accept PayPal.
The item will be shipped within 4 business day after the payment is received.
If payment can't be confirmed within 4 days after the close of the auction, a reminder will be sent to you.

Return Policy
Returns accepted
After receiving the item, contact seller within 3 days.
Please see more details on Shipping and payments tab.

Others

We are officially registered as legal business and we have to comply with all rules and　regulations including tax requirements from the Japanese government.

$377.40 · Good · Rich Tone Music

Daisy Rock Flower PJ Bass - Pink - 2nd Hand Details:

  • Overall Condition: Good

This is a previously loved item and as such may have signs of use. Please note that whilst we do our best to picture our 2nd hand and Ex demo items in detail it is not always possible to picture all marks, scratches, and blemishes on these items. This may be due to the reflective nature of the item or the finish. We do try to give a broad indication of the general condition in the description, but this is just a guide. If you are looking for something in extremely good condition it is advised to call for a detailed talk through of the item before ordering.

If you have any questions about this item or would like to know more about it, please feel free to contact us with any specific questions and a member of our sales staff will get back to you straight away.

Sell Your Gear

As well as buying 2nd Hand items for cash we are also happy to take items as part exchange against all New, Ex Demo or 2nd hand. So, if you have anything to part exchange then please feel free to contact us through the "sell your gear" link above or email us and we will come back with a quote.

Please Note

This is a 2nd hand item and may not come with all originally included items, original components, or original accessories. Hi-tech items may also not include its original software. Please refer to the pictures and notes above for included items.

All our 2nd hand items are fully checked and setup in advance of shipping. We package very well and usually ship next working day unless the item is marked Collection Only. If the item is marked as Collection Only you can still reserve with a deposit using our reserve and collect facility at checkout or by contacting us.

Collection BR5 Orpington/Bromley or may meet half way depending on location.

item.title: Epiphone Jack Casady Signature Fretless Bass — $974.38
item.description:
$974.38 · Very Good · Found Sound

The Jack Casady Signature fretless bass was designed by the man himself, featuring a Casady-designed JCB low impedance pickup paired with a 3 position rotary impedance control to give you tonal variety. It sports a traditional semi-hollow LP Signature Bass body made of layered maple with single ply cream binding, a mahogany neck with a fretless laurel fingerboard with white dot position markers along the top of the board. Controls include the aforementioned 3 position rotary control and a master volume and master tone control.

Features
  • Designed by Rock Hall of Fame legend Jack Casady
  • Features Casady-designed JCB low-impedance pickup
  • Three-position rotary control for tonal versatility
  • Premium gig bag included for easy transport
  • Fretless version for a unique playing experience
  • Layered maple body with single ply cream binding
  • Mahogany neck with C profile for comfortable play
  • Aged Royal Tan finish for a classic look

Includes padded gig bag.

Found Sound Australia Pty Ltd.
110 Albert St
Brunswick East, VIC 3057
Australia

item.title: Ibanez GA35TCE Tobacco Burst w/Gig Bag - Used - As-Is — $199
item.description:
$199 · Good · Mill River Music & Guitar Shop

Ibanez GA35TCE, Tobacco Burst, Gig Bag - Used - As-Is
The GA35TCE is one of the more practical thinline classical guitars on the market — a spruce top over sapele back and sides, a thinline cutaway body that resists feedback and stays comfortable through long sessions, and a Nyatoh neck with a narrower 46mm nut width that makes the transition from steel-string or electric considerably easier than a traditional full-width classical neck. The Ibanez undersaddle pickup paired with the AEQ210T preamp gives you onboard EQ and a built-in tuner, making this a practical stage-ready nylon string instrument.
We are selling this one as-is and want to be completely upfront about why. The bridge saddle is bottomed out and notched, which means the action cannot easily be lowered any further through normal adjustment. As it currently sits, the action is at classical spec and the guitar plays well right now — this is not a guitar that plays poorly today. But a buyer who wants lower action than classical spec, or who runs into changes in the saddle or bridge over time, should know that the usual adjustment path is not available without further work, such as a saddle replacement or bridge service. We are disclosing this clearly because that is how we do things, and it is reflected in the as-is listing and price.
Beyond that specific issue, there are minor signs of cosmetic use consistent with a played instrument. Check the photos for the full picture. Comes with a gig bag.
- Make: Ibanez
- Model: GA35TCE
- Country of origin: Indonesia
- Body style: Thinline cutaway classical
- Top: Spruce, high gloss finish
- Back and sides: Sapele, high gloss finish
- Neck: Nyatoh, 14-fret join
- Fingerboard: Rosewood, offset abalone dot inlays
- Nut width: 46mm (1.81")
- Scale length: 25.5" (648mm)
- Frets: 21
- Saddle: Bottomed out and notched — action cannot be easily lowered, see description
- Nut: Plastic
- Bridge: Rosewood
- Tuners: Gold classical with black knobs
- Pickup: Ibanez Undersaddle
- Preamp: Ibanez AEQ210T with onboard tuner, volume, treble, bass
- Outputs: Balanced XLR and 1/4" stereo
- Strings: Nylon
- Weight: 3 lbs 10 oz
- Modifications: None
- Includes: Gig bag
This item is sold as-is. The bridge saddle limitation described above is a known issue at time of sale.
Ships carefully packed and insured for full value.
International buyers are welcome — please contact us for a shipping quote before purchasing.
About Mill River Music
Mill River Music is an independent, human-powered music shop located in downtown Northampton, MA, run by a team of players, enthusiasts, and certified repair technicians. Every piece of used gear we sell is personally inspected, tested, and set up before it ships — we don't list it until we'd be happy to play it ourselves.
As Martin and Taylor certified techs, we hold our guitar work to a high standard, and we're committed to full transparency about the condition of everything we sell. No surprises.
When you buy from Mill River, you're supporting an independent local business that genuinely cares — about gear, about players, and about doing things right. We're here every step of the way. Reach out anytime.

item.title: Tobias Basic 4 string Bass Pre-Gibson Era - Natural — $3,600
item.description:
$3,600 · Good · Holy One Music

Offered for sale is a rare Pre-Gibson Tobias Basic 4-String Bass, serial number 2016.

Built during the highly sought-after Michael Tobias era, this instrument represents the craftsmanship and design philosophy that established Tobias as one of the most respected boutique bass builders of the late 1980s.

This bass features classic neck-through construction with beautiful Zebrawood body wings, a Maple neck with Purpleheart stringers, and an elegant unbound Rosewood fingerboard without inlays, giving it the understated aesthetic that early Tobias instruments are known for.

Specifications

  • Pre-Gibson Tobias Basic 4
  • Serial Number: #2016
  • Made in the USA
  • Neck-through construction
  • Zebrawood body wings
  • Maple neck with Purpleheart stringers
  • Rosewood fingerboard (unbound, no inlays)
  • Original Bartolini pickups
  • Magnetic wooden control cavity cover

Condition

This bass is being listed as Good Condition and is best described as a well-maintained player's instrument.

The instrument shows honest cosmetic wear consistent with over three decades of use, including various dings, scratches, finish wear, and signs of regular playing.

Please note that the neck/headstock has been professionally repaired at some point in the instrument's life. The repair is stable, and the bass is currently structurally sound, fully functional, and plays as it should. All electronics are functioning properly.

For complete transparency, detailed photographs of the repair area and all cosmetic imperfections have been included in the listing. Please review all photos carefully and feel free to contact me with any questions or requests for additional information.

Despite the repair and cosmetic wear, this bass retains everything that has made early Tobias instruments legendary: exceptional craftsmanship, outstanding playability, and the articulate, responsive tone that only a Michael Tobias-era instrument can deliver.

A fantastic opportunity to own a genuine Pre-Gibson Tobias at a price point well below comparable examples without repairs.

Please feel free to reach out with any questions.

item.title: MUSIC MAN 80s Sabre Bass, Modified Sunburst [C003390] (06/26) — $3,114.53
item.description:
$3,114.53 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

The 80s Music Man SABRE BASS has arrived.
Released shortly after the success of the StingRay, this model can be described as ganother answerh that strongly retains the DNA of Leo Fenderfs design era. It features the classic combination of an ash body and maple neck, equipped with two large humbuckers. Rather than simply prioritizing power, the series/parallel switch and tone circuit allow for a wide range of sound shaping?from robust lows to crisp mids.

The instrument wefve received this time features a 1979 Saber body joined to a 1979 StingRay neck. As a result, the neck joint has been modified from a four-point to a three-point joint. The date stamped on the potentiometers?which appear to be original?is 1981; it is believed that parts replacement and modifications were carried out at gSystem Craft,h a bass specialty shop in Osaka.

The pickups are original, but the volume pot (1986), mini-switch, and jack have been replaced. The mini-switch functions as a pickup selector, allowing for series/parallel switching of the front pickup and series/parallel switching of the rear pickup.
The position of the string guide has been changed, and the strap pins and mute have been replaced. While it shows signs of wear appropriate for its age, there are no issues with its playability.
The nut and frets were replaced after the guitar was received, and the fingerboard was refinished as part of that process.
The rod nut (where the truss rod is adjusted) was stripped, so it was machined to metric specifications and repaired.
Currently, the neck is in good condition, and the truss rod is adjustable.
Condition Grade: B+: Some minor scratches, but no issues for normal use

Serial Number: C003390
Country of Manufacture: USA
Case: Non-original hard case
Weight: 4.58 kg
Truss Rod: No issues; checked up to 60 degrees to the left and right with no problems
Remaining Fret Life: Within normal range; fret height is sufficient for playing
String height (low-string side): 2.5 mm at the 12th fret
String Action (Treble Side): 2.0 mm at the 12th fret
Nut width: 43.0 mm
Neck thickness near the 1st fret: 20.1 mm
Neck thickness near the 12th fret: 24.8 mm
Scale: 864 mm (34")
RADIUS: 184 mm (7.25")
Strings at Setup: D'Addario EXL165 / .045-.105 set
Repairs and Maintenance: Cleaning, string replacement, truss rod adjustment, octave adjustment, action adjustment, electronics repair

* As this is a used item, there may be scratches, dents, and other imperfections that are not clearly visible in the photos or not listed here. Please be aware of this in advance.

* Any listed measurements for the truss rod, remaining fret life, string height, etc., were taken by our staff during setup and are subject to change due to environmental factors such as climate and humidity.
These measurements are intended as a reference at the time of purchase; we cannot guarantee that the measurements will be identical when the item arrives at your location.

item.description:
$941.45 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

The Aerodine Jazz Bass, featuring a cool, aircraft-inspired design, has arrived.
It features an archtop basswood body with a maple neck and rosewood fingerboard, configured with 20 frets.

With an 864mm long scale, itfs equipped with PJ pickups that allow for a wide range of sound shaping, as well as easy-to-use 2-volume, 1-tone controls.

This model beautifully combines the look of a matching headstock, the rich, full-bodied sound of Precision Bass pickups, and a slim Jazz Bass neck, making it suitable for any musical genre.
Overall, there are numerous scratches and dents, with paint peeling at the tip of the headstock and tarnish on the metal parts.
The frets have been dressed and have about 7/10 of their original height remaining; the neck is in good condition, and the truss rod is adjustable.

item.title: STERLING RAY34-MH Black [SR12999] (06/26) — $864.07
item.description:
$864.07 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

This is the gRay34h from the affordable gStarlin by Music Manh brand, which carries on the legacy of Music Manfs popular gStingrayh model.
It faithfully reproduces not only the visual appeal but also the powerful Stingray sound, thanks to its single humbucking pickup and active 3-band EQ.

It features a pickguard with a pearloid pattern.

item.title: Alembic Spoiler 4 string bass 1983 - Natural — $3,000
item.description:
$3,000 · Good · Holy One Music

Offered here is a 1983 Alembic Spoiler 4-String Bass, serial number 83S2694.

This is an early-production Spoiler built during the golden era of Alembic in Santa Rosa, California. Featuring Alembic's renowned neck-through construction, low-impedance pickups, and active filter electronics, this instrument delivers the articulate, hi-fi tone and exceptional sustain that have made Alembic one of the most respected names in boutique bass building.

Specifications

  • Year: 1983
  • Model: Spoiler 4-String
  • Serial Number: 83S2694
  • Scale Length: 32.125" Medium Scale
  • Construction: Neck-Through-Body
  • Body Wood: Mahogany
  • Neck Wood: Maple
  • Fingerboard: Ebony with Oval Inlays
  • Pickups: Original Alembic Low-Impedance Pickups × 2
  • Electronics: Original Alembic Active Electronics
  • Hardware: Original Brass Bridge, Brass Nut, and Tuners
  • Made in Santa Rosa, California, USA

Condition

This bass is being listed as Good Condition due to its age and cosmetic wear, but it is best described as a well-maintained player's instrument.

The finish shows honest play wear accumulated over more than four decades, including finish chips, dings, and areas of cosmetic wear throughout the body. The brass hardware also exhibits natural wear and finish loss consistent with regular use and age. Detailed photographs of all cosmetic imperfections have been provided for full transparency.

Despite the cosmetic wear, the bass remains structurally sound and fully functional. All original Alembic electronics and preamp functions operate exactly as they should, and the instrument plays and sounds fantastic.

This is not a collector-grade closet queen, but rather a genuine vintage Alembic that has been played, enjoyed, and carefully maintained over the years.

A wonderful opportunity to own an early Alembic Spoiler with all the character, tone, and craftsmanship that these instruments are known for.

Please feel free to contact me with any questions or for additional photos.

item.title: 1980s Hohner B2A Headless Bass Guitar Active Pickups Rare Red Finish — $549.99
item.description:
$549.99 · Good · Second Hand Sam Guitars

Description

Step into the world of iconic late-’80s headless bass design with this 1980s Hohner Professional B2A Headless Bass in red. Built under license from Steinberger, this innovative instrument delivers the sleek aesthetics and practical performance that made headless basses a staple of modern bass playing. This is a Hohner B2A, a Steinberger-style bass in an elusive red finish. It features an adjustable stack knob-style preamp that can be adjusted in the back.

Condition

This guitar is in good vintage condition with a buzz on the eighth fret, which can be fixed from a fret recondition. It also has some paint chips, and one of the screws for the tuners has been changed, but this bass still has that vintage 80s feel and style.


Weighs 6.6 lb.

Please review the pictures for more detail.


This guitar will be packed well but a case is not included.

Guitar Condition Ratings

 All our guitars are graded per Reverb’s gear grading guide to help determine the condition of our guitars in a uniform fashion. (We have added the approximate percentage in each category as an additional measure). All of our guitars are preowned; they may show some type of usage and handling wear. All guitars have been tested for functionality and are playable; in some rare instances, a guitar may need a small adjustment or setup. Any outstanding problems will be noted.

 Also note: Condition ratings will only apply to the guitar and not the case. Cases are for protecting the valuable merchandise inside and will always show some kind of wear and tear.

Mint (Approx. 98%) For something to truly be mint, it needs to be just shy of Brand New. Mint items should include the original packaging in most cases. If there’s any sort of imperfection, the item should be listed in Excellent condition at best.

Excellent (91% to 97%) When something’s been used a bit but is still close to new, you can file it under Excellent. For newer gear, Excellent items should be free from blemishes and other visual defects. For vintage items, there may be some marks here and there, but the item is still in the top echelon when compared to similar examples. All Excellent items should be 100% functional in every way.

Very Good (80% to 90%) Very Good describes items with perhaps a few more cosmetic imperfections than Excellent but are still in great condition overall. With a Very Good guitar, for example, you might find some scratches, buckle rash, or other minor blemishes but nothing that affects the playability or function of the instrument.

Good (70% to 79%) Good condition includes items that are in fine working order but have some visual imperfections here and there. A pedal with scratches and dents on its chassis, a guitar with some cracks in the binding, or maybe an old snare drum with some corrosion on the lugs could all be classified as Good. All of these items work just fine but have enough visual imperfections to only be in the middle of the scale.

item.title: Linc Luthier Danger 6 string Fretless 1993 - Natural — $3,000
item.description:
$3,000 · Very Good · Holy One Music

This is a Linc Luthier Danger Bass 6-String Fretless, handcrafted in November 1993 by the American custom luthier brand Linc Luthier.

This instrument features the optional dual Bartolini P4 pickups, fretless fingerboard, and custom hard case.

Linc Luthier is a boutique American workshop that has been building instruments since 1991. Although it is a small custom shop, its instruments have attracted the attention of renowned musicians such as Victor Wooten, Stanley Clarke, Lee Sklar, Lemmy, and even Sir Paul McCartney.

Specifications

  • Body constructed from laminated African Zebrawood, created by resawing and re-laminating solid wood sections
  • Built while preserving the original grain orientation of the wood
  • Laminated Macassar Ebony construction
  • Double Ebony fingerboard
  • Eastern Hard Maple Blister Grain, one-piece
  • One-piece Zebrawood combination
  • Zebrawood lined fretless fingerboard
  • Zebrawood neck heel
  • Zimbabwe Pink Ivory accents
  • Dual Bartolini P4 pickups (Direct Mount)

One of Linc Luthier's most distinctive features is its patented Horizontal Lamination neck construction.

Rather than using conventional laminations, the wood is carefully resawn and reassembled horizontally to restore the original grain orientation. This unique method was designed to provide exceptional neck stability, strong fundamental tone, reduced dead spots, and superior vibration transfer.

The body is also constructed using the same philosophy. Instead of a simple one-piece design, the wood is resawn and re-laminated while maintaining the natural grain direction. According to Linc, this approach improves structural stability while preserving the natural resonance and acoustic characteristics of solid wood, resulting in a more organic and responsive tone.

The dual Bartolini P4 pickups are mounted directly to the body using a Direct Mount system. This design is intended to maximize the transfer of string and body vibrations, preserving as much of the instrument's natural resonance as possible.

A new build with similar specifications currently starts at $4,130 USD, excluding taxes and shipping.

Considering that this is an early-production instrument from 1993, it remains in excellent condition. Aside from a small ding on the headstock, the bass is exceptionally clean and has been very well preserved.

item.title: Player II Mustang® Bass PJ, Rosewood Fingerboard, Aquatone Blue — $569
item.description:
$569 · Excellent · Creeks Tone Parlor

MIM Player II Mustang® Bass PJ in the striking, archive-released Aquatone Blue finish. This stage-ready bass guitar radiates timeless Fender charm.

This bass is exceptionally clean, lightly used, and has been kept in a climate-controlled, gluten/smoke free studio. Perfect for crafting your own sound, it has the looks, tone, and feel that only a Fender delivers.

  • The Body: Classic, resonant solid alder body with a durable gloss polyester finish.
  • Custom Pickguard: Tiki GOD complements the Aquatone Blue finish for a killer surf-punk aesthetic.
  • The Neck: Designed for fast and fluid playability, featuring a Modern “C”-profile with a silky satin urethane finish on the back, a comfy 9.5”-radius slab fingerboard with smooth rolled edges, 19 medium jumbo frets, and a short 30" scale.
  • The Electronics: Player Series Alnico V Split-Coil P bass® and Single-Coil J bass® pickups offer sweet highs, growling mids, and seismic lows that elevate any genre. The 3-way toggle switch lets you easily dial in everything this versatile pickup set has to offer.
  • "Hi-Mass" Bridge: Heavy-duty, high-mass design for improved vibration transfer, enhanced sustain, and tighter low-end.
  • Tuning: Open-gear tuners ensure precise tuning stability for the flexibility to explore endless sonic possibilities.


Cosmetic Disclosure: In the spirit of total transparency and providing honest descriptions, there are two minor cosmetic flaws to note:

  1. Headstock: A couple of small epoxy paint drips on the finish.
  3. Back of Body: A small, rice-sized ding on the alder body.
  4. Both flaws are strictly cosmetic, do not affect the incredible playability or sound, and are barely noticeable from a distance. Please review the close-up photos carefully.


At Creek Johnsons Tone Parlor, we use bulletproof shipping methods. This bass will be packed with elite care, fully insured, and shipped fast to its new home!

item.title: Peavy Cirrus BXP 5 string early 2000’s — $1,127.02
item.description:
$1,127.02 · Good · erroll 's Gear Outlet


Peavey Cirrus BXP 5 Walnut w/ Genuine USA Cirrus Preamp Upgrade + Flight Case

Rare Peavey Cirrus BXP 5-string in Walnut finish, upgraded with genuine USA Peavey Cirrus electronics.

This is not a stock BXP. The original preamp has been replaced with an authentic USA Cirrus preamp, sourced from the United States and professionally installed. The result is more headroom, punch, tonal flexibility and definition while keeping the characteristic Cirrus sound.

Specifications

  • Peavey Cirrus BXP 5-string
  • Rare Walnut finish
  • Neck-through construction
  • Genuine USA Peavey Cirrus preamp upgrade
  • Active electronics
  • VFL active pickups
  • Low action
  • Fast, comfortable neck
  • Fully functional and ready to play

Condition

Very good overall condition for its age, with normal signs of use. The bass is technically 100%: no noise, crackling or electronic issues.

One tuning machine has been replaced with a Warwick tuner.

Included:

  • Non-original flight case
  • Original Peavey BXP preamp
  • Spare Warwick tuner
  • Spare bridge parts
  • Alternative coloured control knobs

Why This One Is Special

A Walnut neck-through Cirrus is already hard to find, especially in Europe. This one has also been upgraded with genuine USA Cirrus electronics, which gives it a stronger low end, more usable midrange control and extra clarity compared to a standard BXP.

It cuts through a mix, but still stays musical thanks to the VFL active pickups and the natural warmth of the Walnut body.

This is not a mod-project. It is a serious, professionally upgraded player’s bass.

Trade

Possible trade within the Netherlands for a Sire V7 5-string or Sadowsky MetroExpress JJ 5-string.

Shipping within the Netherlands only.

The bass will be packed securely and shipped in the included flight case. Pickup is also possible.

item.description:

Description from Fender: During the Red Hot Chili Peppers' first success in the alt-rock scene, a fan presented Flea with a 1961 Fender Jazz Bass, whose "old wood sound" quickly made it one of his favorite instruments. It's the bass sound that's all over Stadium Arcadium and The Getaway. If you've ever seen him live, then you know Flea plays hard, and his beloved '61 J Bass has taken a real beating over the years. Fender managed to capture every nuance of this iconic Shell Pink bass in painstaking detail, so the Flea Road Worn Jazz Bass doesn't just look worn in, it feels like an old friend when you first pick it up.

item.title: Fender Fender Custom Shop Reggie Hamilton Jazz Bass V 2022 - BLACK — $4,500
item.description:
$4,500 · Very Good · Choi’s Gear


Fender Custom Shop Reggie Hamilton Jazz Bass V Black 2022


Artist Signature Series by Fender

The world’s greatest artists have come to Fender Custom Shop for decades in search of their perfect instruments. Artfully crafting meticulous replicas using the same techniques and toolings used to create the originals, Fender offers the same artist instruments to you, built in the same shop by the same skilled craftsmen.

Premium Features of the Fender Custom Shop Reggie Hamilton Signature

The Custom Shop Reggie Hamilton Signature Jazz Bass V is equipped with a Custom Noiseless™ single-coil bridge pickup and a American Standard Alnico V split single-coil p-bass middle pickup, along with active electronics and extensive controls. This configuration helps to produce era-specific authenticity along while along with modern versatility and reliability that is perfect for on stage and in the studio. Other premium features include real white shell block inlays on an unbound neck, graphite neck support rods, a sculpted heel that provides easier acccess to the upper frets, Hipshot® Bass Xtender drop A tuning key on the B string, graphite neck support rods, a Schaller® straplock system and a custom 5-bolt neck plate.

This signature model comes complete with a three-colour sunburst and nitrocellulose lacquer finish, plus a range of Custom Shop accessories and a Custom Shop certificate of authenticity.

Unrivaled Quality and Craftsmanship

Since 1987, the skilled craftsmen in Fender’s Corona, California ”dream factory” have been producing guitars that are known worldwide for their artistry and unrivaled quality. Ranging from the fine to the fantastic, whether it’s building a new custom instrument from the ground up or modifying an existing one, their best is the best. These wonderful instruments have been crafted for and with the input of the world’s finest guitarists such as Clapton, Springsteen, Cray, Gilmour, Townshend, Wilko Johnson and Stevie Ray Vaughan, to name just a few.

The History of the Jazz Bass

Introduced in 1960, Fender welcomed a new decade by debuting a deluxe bass guitar, the Jazz Bass. Sleek and supercharged with dual pickups, a slim neck and a curvaceous offset waist, the Jazz Bass has ruled the world of electric bass ever since Fender first introduced it. The versatile and unmistakable tone, effortless feel and performance make the Jazz Bass the number-one choice for professional bassists worldwide.

item.title: Hayman 4040 Bass c 1970 natural original vintage uk England Dallas arbiter sound city — $1,995
item.description:
$1,995 · Good · The Bonfires Vintage

Worldwide Shipping!

Original vintage c 1972 Hayman 4040 Bass in gorgeous playing condition!

These Haymans are some of the rarest UK made basses from the good old days, and were Arbiter’s (sound city) music store’s house brand, designed by Bob Pearson!

They are unique in every way, from the “superflux” pickups, to see through headstock inlay, plexiglass pickguard, zero fret, (very Jim Burns) and just wackadoo everything!

This fine example plays beautifully; arrow neck, excellent frets, and those pickups are HOT(!) and absolutely magical. She’s just been set up with flat 100’s, and is lightning in the hands.

Lots of natural patina, lacquer and play wear; all superficial, all included at no extra charge. Tons of cool spidering and wear in the body clear coat! -she ships in the hard case pictured. Enjoy!

item.title: Squier Squier Classic Vibe Bass VI Antigua CME Exclusive Limited Edition with Hard Case — $699
item.description:
$699 · Excellent · Leonardo's Gear Outlet

Squier Classic Vibe Bass VI Antigua CME Exclusive Limited Edition with Hard Case

Limited-edition CME exclusive Antigua finish, completely unplayed with all tags and paperwork, included with a fitted hardshell case. Original owner, bought new from Chicago Music Exchange.

Description:

Squier Classic Vibe Bass VI in the limited Antigua finish, the Chicago Music Exchange exclusive run. This is the sought-after Antigua burst with the maple fingerboard, black block inlays, and black neck binding, a finish with a real cult following that rarely comes up unplayed.

This one is in excellent condition and completely unplayed. I bought it new from Chicago Music Exchange, original owner, and it has never been played, not a single gig or session. All hang tags, the certificate, and paperwork are still with it, and the tremolo arm is included.

The Bass VI is the short-scale six-string that sits between a guitar and a bass, tuned an octave lower, with three Fender-designed alnico single-coil pickups, individual on/off slide switches, the bass-cut strangle circuit, and the vintage-style floating tremolo. Great for studio textures, baritone parts, surf, and low-end doubling.

Condition: Mint and unplayed. No wear, no marks, no fret wear, everything as it left the shop. Fully functional. See photos, which show it new with the case candy and tags still attached.

Included:

  • Squier Classic Vibe Bass VI, Antigua (CME limited exclusive)
  • Tremolo arm
  • All original tags, certificate, and paperwork
  • Fitted hardshell case (these did not ship with a case, so this is a genuine add). The bass sits properly in the case, shown in the photos.

Why I'm selling: I picked it up, never ended up using it, and would rather it go to someone who will play it. It has sat stored and protected in the case since I got it.

Shipping and pickup: Local pickup welcome in the Miami / Aventura area. For buyers outside the area, I ship safely in the included hardshell case through Reverb. Message me with your location for a shipping quote if needed.

Price is $699 or best offer.

item.title: Baldwin Burns Baby Bison Bass Guitar - All Original - Clean Poly Finish - Rare Palm Mute and Original OHSC included - 1966 — $889
item.description:
$889 · Very Good · Tom C.'s East Coast Spot

I am selling my Baldwin Burns Baby Bison Bass guitar, manufactured in England around 1965 and sold in the US by Baldwin. This bass is in very good condition, is all original, with a clean glossy polyurethane finish, very few nicks or scrapes, and a mostly pretty neck with some relief. The bridge screws are most likely stripped so I have made a workaround for height. The frets are in playable condition and the gear box truss rod is working. Comes in the original Burns/Baldwin hard case.

Things to note are the following: 

- The neck double coil pickup works and sounds full, the bridge pickup does not work. 

- The tiny screws which raise and lower the bridge may be stripped or may just need the exact hex tool, which I do not have. I have added two dimes to get the action comfortable for playing and this could be adjusted. It is not the best designed bridge system but is original.

- The zero fret has some wear on the g string side (pictured) and there is buzz if you pluck the first fret on the G string too hard. I have not worked with the truss rod much except to be sure it turns (Hex tool included) and have not taken the neck off to add or remove a shim. Both could resolve this as the frets look clean and the neck is pretty straight. Notes play across the fretboard with some buzz if you pick hard around the 14th fret on some of the strings.

- The often missing bridge mute is included and the foam is still somewhat soft on it. I do not use it but you may find it to your liking. One of the screws is slightly bent but it does screw into place.

- After I took these pictures I was cleaning the case and found a small chip that had come off the upper horn. Must have been in there for a decade or two - it has been reglued and can be seen in the tight shot in the case and one of the later shots of the horn.

The controls on this guitar, and other Burns from the mid sixties (Vibraslim, Baby Bison guitar) work a bit differently than normal. The three way switch is standard, the first knob towards the strings is volume, the middle knob is presence (it dials in the second coil on the neck pickup) and the third is tone. The effect of the presence knob is to make a less bright tone (to my ear) and the tone knob allows for more tonal variation. I have not seen a bass pickup of this type - the “Bar-O-Matics” - available online but have seen, and own, a number of 6 string versions. I’ve considered putting one into the bridge position but would rather leave it intact for you the buyer to decide. With the presence and tone options there are a few variations in sound to work with as is.

This is a substantial bass with a medium to wide neck - yet also has a comfortable 30 inch scale. Nut width is 1.75 inches. From the tip of the headstock to the bottom it is 45 inches long and the bout is about 15 inches across. It is hefty and weighs around 9.5 pounds.

The truss rod gear box (the same one in Gretsch guitars via the sale of Burns and Gretsch to Baldwin between 1965 and 1967) at the base of the neck turns at a very high 16:1 ratio, which means that about 4 turns seems to needed to do a bit of adjustment. I loosened the neck about a few turns recently and the truss rod has much more adjustment in it if for some reason you want to tinker.

Important note: The gear box turns opposite of what is “normal”. To the left is tightening, to the right is loosening. Feel free to look this up - I did and was surprised. The patented truss rod key is missing (I did not receive it when I bought the bass) but I learned that a spanner insert bit, 1/4" Hex Shank works well on these guitars. I have an extra so will include it.

The heavy polyester finish on this guitar is remarkably clean - often they are weathered and creased by the change in environment after being shipped overseas. The original Van Ghent tuners work smoothly and keep tune.

It seems I replaced the original jack at some point, the old jack is included. I have also included an image of string height @ 1st, 6th, 12th fret positions on both sides of the fretboard. There is no room to include the individual shots, will send directly if you would like. I have included an image of the pots, wiring and gear box stem.

I do not remember what strings I put on it when I changed them a year or so ago. It once had black taped roto-matic strings which sounded and felt great.

The original Baldwin case is included - it is solid and in very good shape.

I’ve described this 60 year old bass guitar as best as I can with words and images, if you have any questions be sure to ask me before you buy.

Will pack professionally and ship quickly. It is a heavy and large case so will let Reverb figure out the shipping cost.

item.title: Godin A4 Ultra Fretted Bass - Semi Acoustic/Electric with Synth Access — $1,396.13
item.description:
$1,396.13 · Very Good · Full Rack Outlet

Great condition. Just has a little ding in one spot of the finish near the binding by the input jack, see photos. Comes with Godin bag.

US shipping includes all customs fees.

-

The A4 Ultra SA semi-acoustic bass feature more sonic possibilities than ever with the addition of a Low Profile Lace Sensor electric pickup with side mounted volume and tone controls.

Other features include Custom Godin electronics with individual saddle transducers in the bridge, custom preamp section voiced specifically for the A4 Ultra SA as well as output and 13-pin capabilities for computer and synth access. Incredible acoustic bass sounds are available through the output jack and controlling your tone is made easy with the Custom Godin Preamp section. The two-chambered body design found in all of the A-Series instruments is also at work in the A4 Ultra. This design resists feedback as well as the negative effects that loud stage levels can have on top vibration.

Specs:
• Rock Maple neck
• Rosewood Fingerboard on Fretted Version
• 16" fingerboard radius
• 34" Scale
• 1 1/2" nut width
• Two-Chamber, Silver Leaf Maple body
• Solid Spruce Top
• Lace Sensor, low profile electric pickup
• Side mounted volume & tone controls for electric pickup 
• Custom Godin Saddle Transducers and custom preamp
• Volume, Mid, Treble and Bass controls
• Natural Semi-Gloss
